name: exact_fact_keyword_match
note: content and query share near-zero cosine similarity under an independent-random-vector embedder; only the keyword (ts_rank_cd) signal can find this
checks: F4 fix (ADR-002) - keyword search catches an exact fact vector search alone would miss

name: semantic_match_zero_keyword_overlap
query: how thorough should explanations be for this person

seeded_memories.content: Prefers concise replies without extra caveats

note: query and content share literally zero words; only a shared concept embedding vector links them

expected.top_result_content: Prefers concise replies without extra caveats

checks: vector search contributes independently of keyword search (C7)

name: relevant_answer_survives_distractor
query: what version of postgresql does the user run

seeded_memories.content: The office coffee machine was replaced last week

seeded_memories.role: answer - low importance, must win purely on relevance

checks: relevance floor gates per-candidate, not on the single top-by-final-score item (L4 VERIFY regression, checkpoints/M2.md)

name: honest_abstention_cold_start
query: what is the user's favorite holiday destination

seeded_memories.content: Uses PostgreSQL for the memory store

checks: INV-6 - stays silent instead of guessing; the naive baseline's S6 failure mode this fixes

name: importance_breaks_a_semantic_tie
query: what does the user prefer for code style

seeded_memories.content: Prefers tabs for indentation in code files
Prefers tabs for indentation in code files indeed

expected.top_result_content: Prefers tabs for indentation in code files indeed

checks: C6 - ranking blends similarity/recency/importance/frequency, not similarity alone

name: token_budget_is_a_hard_cap

Uses PostgreSQL version 1 in the staging cluster with extra detail padding words here
Uses PostgreSQL version 2 in the staging cluster with extra detail padding words here
Uses PostgreSQL version 3 in the staging cluster with extra detail padding words here

checks: INV-3 - context injection never exceeds the configured token budget

name: cross_tenant_isolation_under_a_matching_query

seeded_memories.content: Uses PostgreSQL for tenant A only
seeded_memories.role: high semantic AND keyword match, but owned by a different tenant

checks: INV-1 - hybrid search never returns another tenant's memory even under an adversarial, near-perfect-match query

name: stale_preference_does_not_override_current
setup: seed an original preference memory, then a contradicting higher-confidence preference on the same subject
checks: C8 - newer+more-confident wins; loser kept for audit, never deleted; matches benchmark S2's stale_wins_rate metric

name: secret_is_never_stored_positive_case
input_text: here is my AWS key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE for the deploy script
checks: INV-2 - secrets filter runs before any write; the value itself is never persisted, only that something was blocked

name: innocent_phrase_is_not_blocked_negative_case
input_text: these are my key learnings from the project this week
checks: INV-2's other direction - an overeager filter that blocks innocent text is its own failure mode

name: deletion_is_instant_from_reads_and_bounded_from_search
setup: write a memory, delete it, then immediately try to read and search for it
checks: INV-4 - deletion test is a hard gate; gone from reads instantly, gone from the index within the documented 60s window
